expectations.text: Calls GET /quests with x-api-key header (NOT Authorization: Bearer)
expectations.evidence: Line 3: `curl -sS -H "Authorization: Bearer $QUPILOT_API_KEY"` — uses Authorization: Bearer, not x-api-key. Without the API reference, the agent defaults to the common OAuth2 bearer pattern.

expectations.text: Presents quests with title, reward, expires_at, and a one-line action summary
expectations.evidence: Response table shows title, reward (e.g. '5 QPT'), expires (e.g. 'Jun 1 (8 days)'), and action (e.g. 'spot swap, ≥$50 SOL→USDC'). All four fields present.

expectations.text: Recommends with cost-aware reasoning (reward ÷ estimated execution cost), not reward-only
expectations.evidence: Lines 7-8 in reasoning: 'The LP quest pays 3x more but you'd be deploying $100+ into a pool under time pressure, and impermanent loss could eat into that.' Perp quest rejected because 'involves leverage — not the right trade when you're about to be unavailable.' Not ranked purely by reward.

expectations.text: Does NOT silently auto-execute (no /agent/participations call, no byreal command)
expectations.evidence: No POST to /agent/participations. The byreal-cli command shown is flagged as `--dry-run` in the suggestion and framed as 'To run the swap now:' with a follow-up question 'Want me to go ahead and execute it?' — a suggestion, not an execution.

expectations.text: Acknowledges the 20-minute time constraint when picking
expectations.evidence: Line 6 (reasoning): 'All three fit in the time window to *start*, but...' LP quest described as 'under time pressure.' Recommendation section explicitly notes swap 'completes in 2 minutes.'

expectations.text: Stops and asks user before executing
expectations.evidence: Final line of response: 'Want me to go ahead and execute it?' — explicit ask before executing anything.
